title: rhinanthus vernalis subsp. aestivalis
intro: Rhinanthus vernalis subsp. aestivalis, also known as the autumn broomrape, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rhinanthus vernalis subsp. aestivalis are said to include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and aiding in the treatment of various respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for use in herbal remedies through infusion, tincture, or decoction, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
